actors.name: Donald Trump
actors.blurb: Donald Trump remains the central figure in the U.S. political news cycle, with his administration's policies and statements driving significant coverage. His recent actions include demanding the firing of late-night host Jimmy Kimmel, engaging in diplomatic tensions with Iran, and making controversial claims about military victories. Trump's influence extends to domestic and international issues, including economic policies, military engagements, and his ongoing feuds with political opponents and media figures.

Meloni's leadership is also scrutinized for her handling of domestic issues and her alignment with other right-wing leaders globally.

actors.name: Péter Magyar
actors.blurb: Péter Magyar has emerged as a key political figure in Hungary following his party's victory in the recent elections, ending Viktor Orbán's long-standing rule. His actions and statements regarding the formation of a new government, the establishment of new ministries, and his diplomatic engagements with the EU have generated significant media coverage. Magyar's focus on anti-corruption and governance reforms is central to his political agenda.

actors.name: Jordan Bardella
actors.role: President of the National Rally (Rassemblement National), France
actors.blurb: Jordan Bardella's leadership of France's far-right National Rally continues to shape the political landscape in France. His recent activities include participating in May Day rallies, addressing internal party dynamics with Marine Le Pen, and taking stances on international issues such as Israel's actions against Hezbollah. Bardella's statements and policy positions have drawn both support and criticism, making him a central figure in French political news.

actors.name: Santiago Abascal
actors.blurb: Santiago Abascal remains a prominent figure in Spanish politics as the leader of the far-right Vox party. His recent activities include launching election campaigns in Andalusia, criticizing government policies on immigration and legal security, and engaging in coalition negotiations with the People's Party (PP). Abascal's leadership and policy stances continue to drive significant media coverage and political debate in Spain.
